title: DesignSpace
description: Optional design-space layer over a ProcessGraph. It is additive and snake_case. Two parts: (1) element-level annotations on existing nodes/edges/flows (quantitative parameters with uncertainty + optional decision domains, node variants, flow economics, edge change-moves); (2) a top-level `design_space` object that flattens the declared variables, objectives, and budgets into one artifact an LLM converts into a search/optimization tool. It does not need to be directly solver-parseable; it is structured so that conversion is easy. Searching the space and scoring candidates are done by the external tool, not here.

quantity.description: A loose numeric value with a unit. `value` may be null while still being mapped.

uncertainty.description: Per-quantitative-parameter uncertainty (a value we do not control or do not know exactly). Distinct from `variable_domain` (a value we choose). A parameter may carry both.

percent.description: true if plus_minus is a percentage

variable_domain.description: The set of values the external search may choose for a decision variable.

quantitative_parameter.description: A property/attribute value promoted from a plain string to a structured number. A node/edge `attributes`/`properties` value may be EITHER a plain string (free-text, default) OR this object.

property_value.description: What a node `attributes` value or edge `properties` value may be once the design-space layer is adopted: a plain string (as today) or a quantitative_parameter.

node_variant_fields.description: Optional fields a NODE may carry to model an investment alternative. A variant is a separate node whose differing values live in its own attributes; adopting it carries a one-off cost/time.

variant_of.description: id of the base node this is a variant of

flow_economics.description: Optional economics a FLOW (inside an edge's flows[]) may carry so the external search can assess profitability and keep-vs-eliminate.

edge_change.description: Optional `change` object on an EDGE describing the design moves available on it and their change cost/time.

objective.description: A dimension the external search optimizes or must satisfy.

budget.description: A limit the search must respect (e.g. total change cost or time).

variable_ref.description: One entry in design_space.variables — a flattened pointer to where variability lives, so the LLM has a single list of the move set. One of: a parameter range, a node-variant choice, or a flow move.

options.description: node ids (base + its variants) the search chooses among

design_space.description: The exported handoff artifact. Flattens the in-graph annotations into one place: the decision variables/moves, the objectives, and the budgets. An LLM converts this (plus the graph) into the actual search tool.

base_graph_id.description: the as-is graph this space explores around

notes.description: free text guidance for the LLM->tool conversion
